title: ipomoea digitata var. quinquefida
intro: Ipomoea digitata var. quinquefida, commonly known as the Five-Leaf Morning Glory, is a plant species within the Convolvulaceae family. It is a perennial vine with distinctive five-lobed leaves and is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. The Five-Leaf Morning Glory can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
